summary: Backend testing for The Girl House e-commerce API. 42/43 pytest tests passed (97.7%). All critical flows verified: auth+cookies, admin protection, product CRUD, filters/search/sort, order creation with COD/coupon/delivery-fee/stock-decrement, public order lookup, status transitions, dashboard stats, settings, coupon WELCOME10 (10% percent), Stripe placeholder 501. ONE critical bug: brute-force lockout (5-fail 429) does not trigger because request.client.host returns the load-balancer proxy IP and there are multiple proxy IPs in front of the backend, so the per-IP+email counter splits across proxies and never reaches 5.

critical.issue: Brute-force lockout never triggers under real ingress. Identifier uses request.client.host which is the immediate proxy IP. Logs show login_attempts split across at least 2 proxy IPs (10.64.134.179 and 10.64.128.142) for the same email, so the count per record never reaches 5 -> 429 not returned. Security risk: unbounded password guessing.

minor.issue: Spec asked to verify slug 'dm-girl-house' but no such product is seeded (only 12 products with slugs like balea-hair-repair-mask, langhaarmadchen-hair-oil, etc). Returns 404. Likely a typo in the testing spec; functionally correct.
Auth cookies set with secure=False. Acceptable for preview but should be True in production (env-driven).

action_items: Fix brute-force lockout: use X-Forwarded-For (first IP) or rate-limit by email only. Example: ip = request.headers.get('x-forwarded-for','').split(',')[0].strip() or request.client.host
Optionally seed a product with slug 'dm-girl-house' if the spec really wants it, or update the spec/test wording.
Set cookie secure=True when serving over HTTPS (env-controlled COOKIE_SECURE).
critical_code_review_comments: server.py is ~1140 lines - acceptable for MVP but consider splitting into routers (auth, products, orders, admin, settings) when feature set grows.
Brute-force identifier should not rely on request.client.host behind a reverse proxy.
delete_category/delete_product/delete_coupon return {'deleted': 0} with HTTP 200 even when nothing was deleted - prefer 404 for consistency.
create_order updates stock without checking availability; could go negative under concurrent orders. Consider atomic conditional update {'stock': {'$gte': qty}} and reject when match fails.
Coupon usage increment is not protected by an atomic check on max_uses (race condition).
JWT_SECRET fallback removed (good) but ADMIN_PASSWORD has a default 'Admin@123' fallback - production hardening should require it.
Pydantic models with extra='ignore' silently drop unknown fields - fine, but watch for typos.
seed_data_creation: Created TEST_-prefixed orders/products/coupons during tests; admin product was deleted; some TEST_ orders remain in DB (acceptable for further testing).
context_for_next_testing_agent: All endpoints in scope are wired correctly. Only failing test is TestBruteForce::test_zzz_brute_force_lockout - root cause is multi-proxy ingress masking the real client IP. Tests are idempotent (use unique uuids) and safe to re-run. To re-run: pytest /app/backend/tests/backend_test.py -v
rca of the issue: Brute-force lockout: db.login_attempts is keyed by (proxy_ip + email). Kubernetes ingress load-balances incoming requests across multiple proxy pods, so the same client's failed attempts get distributed across multiple identifier records, none reaching the threshold of 5. Reproduction: hit /api/auth/login with the same wrong credentials 8+ times - status stays 401. DB inspection shows two records with counts 3 and 5 split across IPs 10.64.128.142 and 10.64.134.179. Mitigation: derive client IP from X-Forwarded-For header (first hop) or simply key the counter by email only (or both).
